name: Maricopa County

actions.title: (Mesa) Approval of large-scale data center campus by Aligned Data Centers
actions.summary: The City of Mesa approved site plans for Aligned Data Centers to develop a major data center campus in the Elliot Road Technology Corridor, continuing Mesa's push to become a regional data center hub.

actions.title: (Goodyear) QTS Data Centers expansion approval
actions.summary: The City of Goodyear approved additional data center development by QTS (a Digital Realty company) within the PV303 master-planned business park, with associated utility infrastructure agreements.

actions.title: (Mesa) Data center water-use concerns and sustainability review
actions.summary: Mesa officials and residents raised concerns about the water consumption of new data centers amid Arizona's drought conditions, prompting city discussions on requiring water-efficiency commitments from data center operators.

contextBlurb: Maricopa County, home to Phoenix, Mesa, and Goodyear, is one of the fastest-growing data center markets in the U.S. Multiple cities within the county are competing for hyperscale campus developments while grappling with water scarcity and energy infrastructure demands. Community concerns about water consumption and utility rates have begun shaping policy discussions.

name: Pinal County

actions.title: (Coolidge) Data center development and water-supply agreements
actions.summary: The City of Coolidge continued approving large-scale data center developments in Pinal County, including water-supply agreements despite regional groundwater concerns in the area south of Phoenix.

contextBlurb: Pinal County, located between Phoenix and Tucson, has emerged as an attractive location for data center developers seeking lower land costs. However, the region faces acute groundwater challenges, and new developments have sparked debate about sustainable water allocation for industrial uses.
